stem: A agriculture study measures the yield per hectare (tonnes) for a sample of 15 farms. The sample mean is 174.4 tonnes with a sample standard deviation of 51.8 tonnes. However, 3 of the 15 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 12 observations have a mean of 164.2 tonnes and standard deviation of 33.0 tonnes. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 174.4, 95% CI = [145.7, 203.1]. Without outliers: mean = 164.2, 95% CI = [145.1, 183.3]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [145.1, 203.1]. Point estimate: 169.3 tonnes.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [145.1, 203.1].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
